# JavaScript/TypeScript Code Documentation Standards

## Overview

This document provides guidance for documenting JavaScript and TypeScript code.

## Base Documentation Standards

These Base Standards apply whenever you are creating or updating documentation for any JavaScript or TypeScript file.

0. **Declare Use Of Base Documentation Standards**: Before doing any work, confirm that you are using these base standards.
1. **Use JSDoc**: Wherever possible, use JSDoc comments so intellisense and document generators can accurately pick up documentation.
2. **High Level Documentation**: Ensure that modules/files include high-level documentation about their purpose.

## TypeScript Documentation Standards

These TypeScript Documentation Standards apply whenever you are creating or updating documentation for any TypeScript file.

0. **Declare Use Of TypeScript Documentation Standards**: Before doing any work, confirm that you are using these TypeScript standards.
1. **Describe Types and Interfaces**: Ensure that all `type` and `interface` declarations include documentation that describes their purpose.
2. **Document All Type and Interface Properties**: Ensure that **ALL** properties in declared types and interfaces are documented, even when their meanings may be implicit (such as `name`, `id`, etc).
